bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. All issues have the same utility structure where valueE gives 1.0 utility, so my ideal bid is valueE for every issue. This gives me a utility of 1.0 * (0.1 + 0.1 + 0.2 + 0.3 + 0.3) = 1.0. Starting high leaves room to concede later.

The opponent opened with all valueA (minimum utility for me: 0.2 across all issues), which is their best offer for themselves. It's very early (t=0.02), so I should stay near my ideal bid. I'll maintain high values on my most important issues (issueD and issueE, weight 0.3 each) and make a tiny concession on lower-weight issues to signal willingness to negotiate without giving much away.

The opponent is holding firm at all-valueA (utility 0.2 for me), showing no concession at all. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my top utility while making small concessions. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I'll hold firm there. I can concede slightly on issueA and issueB (low weight 0.1 each) and make a small move on issueC. I'll offer a bid slightly below my last one to show willingness to negotiate while maintaining high utility.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their all-valueA offer (utility 0.2 for me) with no concession whatsoever. It's very early (t=0.06), so I have plenty of time and should not rush to concede. I'll hold firm on my high-priority issues (issueD and issueE) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues (issueA, issueB) to signal willingness to negotiate. I'll target around 0.82 utility by keeping issueD and issueE at valueE, dropping issueC slightly, and conceding more on issueA and issueB.

The opponent is stubbornly holding at all-valueA (utility 0.2 for me) across every round, showing zero concession. It's still very early (t=0.08), so I don't need to panic. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I should protect those. I'll concede a bit more on the low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) and slightly on issueC, while keeping issueD and issueE at valueE to maintain high utility around 0.76.

The opponent has been completely rigid, offering all-valueA every single time (utility 0.2 for me). They haven't conceded at all. We're only at t=0.1, so there's no need to panic yet. I've been conceding on issueA and issueB (low weight issues) while holding firm on issueD and issueE (high weight). I should continue holding firm on my high-weight issues (D and E) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. Let me try conceding issueC a bit more while keeping D and E at valueE.

The opponent is stubbornly holding at all-valueA (utility 0.2 for me) every single round without any concession. It's still very early (t=0.12), so I don't need to rush. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I should protect those. I've been conceding on issueA, issueB, and issueC already. Let me hold firm on issueD and issueE at valueE while giving issueC a slight concession to valueA (since it's only 0.2 weight), keeping issueA and issueB at valueA. This gives me utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.30+0.30 = 0.68, which is a reasonable mid-game offer while protecting my most important issues.

The opponent has been completely rigid, offering all valueA every single turn. My most important issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I need to hold firm on those. I've already conceded issueA, issueB, and issueC to valueA. My current bid gives utility: 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.2 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.30+0.30 = 0.68. Since we're still early (t=0.14) and the opponent shows zero concession, I'll hold my position on issueD and issueE while keeping the low-weight issues at valueA. I'll slightly vary by keeping issueD at valueE and issueE at valueE to maintain my high utility on the most important issues.

Since the opponent shows zero concession, I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (D and E) while keeping low-weight issues at valueA to signal willingness. I'll maintain this position since we're still early (t=0.16) and there's no reason to concede further yet.

The opponent has been completely rigid, always offering all valueA across every issue. The opponent has been completely rigid, always offering all valueA across every issue for 12 rounds. My utility for their offer is 0.2 across all issues = 0.2 total. I've been conceding heavily on issueA, issueB, issueC already (all at valueA), and I've been conceding on issueD. My current bid gives me utility: issueA(0.1×0.2) + issueB(0.1×0.2) + issueC(0.2×0.2) + issueD(0.3×0.6) + issueE(0.3×1.0) = 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.18+0.30 = 0.56. I should continue conceding on issueD (my next step would be valueB) while holding issueE at valueE since it's my highest weight issue. This gives utility: 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.12+0.30 = 0.50, still well above the opponent's all-valueA offer of 0.2.
